steps.title: Find the square side
steps.content:
For a square with side length $s$, the diagonal is $s\sqrt{2}$. Since the diagonal is $204$,
$$
204=s\sqrt{2}
$$
$$
s=\frac{204}{\sqrt{2}}=102\sqrt{2}
$$

steps.title: Find the circle radius
steps.content:
An inscribed circle has diameter equal to the square's side length, so the radius is half the side length:
$$
r=\frac{102\sqrt{2}}{2}=51\sqrt{2}
$$
